div1.entryFree:
Lavish, adj. 1) unrestrained, licentious: when means and l. manners meet together, H4B IV, 4, 64. his l. tongue, H6A II, 5, 47. curbing his l. spirit, Mcb. I, 2, 57.
2) profuse; used of things and persons: let her have needful, but not l. means, Meas. II, 2, 24. had I so l. of my presence been, H4A III, 2, 39.
